content.text:
I'm ready to help you explore the codebase and design implementation plans! 

Let me start by getting familiar with the current state of the project.

<function_calls>
<invoke name="bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /home/filip/CRM && pwd</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<parameter name="command">cd /home/filip/CRM && find . -type f -name "*.py" -o -name "*.ts" -o -name "*.js" -o -name "*.json" | grep -E "\.(py|ts|js|json)$" | head -20</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<parameter name="command">cd /home/filip/CRM && ls -la</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<function_calls>
<invoke name="bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /home/filip/CRM && find . -maxdepth 2 -type f \( -name "*.py" -o -name "*.md" -o -name "*.txt" -o -name "*.json" \) 2>/dev/null | head -30</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<function_calls>
<invoke name="bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /home/filip/CRM && tree -L 2 -I '__pycache__|*.pyc|node_modules' 2>/dev/null || find . -maxdepth 2 -type d | head -20</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<function_calls>
<invoke name="read_file">
<parameter name="path">/home/filip/CRM/app/api/products.py</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<function_calls>
<invoke name="bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /home/filip/CRM && git log --oneline -10</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>
<function_calls>
<invoke name="bash">
<parameter name="command">cd /home/filip/CRM && git diff HEAD app/api/products.py 2>/dev/null | head -50</parameter>
</invoke>
</function_calls>

Great! I'm warmed up and ready to explore your CRM codebase. I can see:

- **Modified file**: `/home/filip/CRM/app/api/products.py` 
- **Untracked directory**: `crm/`
- **Recent commit**: "test" (420de8e)
- **Project type**: Python-based CRM application

I'm now ready to help you with planning and designing implementations. Just provide me with:
1. The requirements for what you want to build
2. (Optional) A specific perspective or approach you'd like me to take

I'll explore the codebase thoroughly, understand existing patterns, and create a detailed implementation plan without modifying any files.
